Details: Chlorine is ineffective in removing the following acetochlor degradates: ESA, OXA, hydroxyacetochlor, deschloroacetochlor, 2-chloro-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, 2-hydroxy-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, and 2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide. Based on data from three studies, these contaminants were removed from -76 to 16 percent when only chlorine was used, or when chlorine was used concurrently with conventional treatment.  Results from one study showed that degradates lacking the acetanilide functional group (2-ethyl-6-methylaniline) were removed completely, suggesting this functional group provides some level of protection against oxidation from free chlorine.
